lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਬਾ ਜੁਗਤਾ ਜੀਉ ਜੁਗਹ ਜੁਗ ਜੋਗੀ ਪਰਮ ਤੰਤ ਮਹਿ ਜੋਗੰ ॥
lines.translit: baabaa jugataa jeeo jugah jug jogee param tant meh jogan |
lines.english: O father, the soul which is united in union as a Yogi, remains united in the supreme essence throughout the ages.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਨਿਰੰਜਨ ਪਾਇਆ ਗਿਆਨ ਕਾਇਆ ਰਸ ਭੋਗੰ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: amrit naam niranjan paaeaa giaan kaaeaa ras bhogan |1| rahaau |
lines.english: One who has obtained the Ambrosial Naam, the Name of the Immaculate Lord - his body enjoys the pleasure of spiritual wisdom. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਵ ਨਗਰੀ ਮਹਿ ਆਸਣਿ ਬੈਸਉ ਕਲਪ ਤਿਆਗੀ ਬਾਦੰ ॥
lines.translit: siv nagaree meh aasan baisau kalap tiaagee baadan |
lines.english: In the Lord's City, he sits in his Yogic posture, and he forsakes his desires and conflicts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿੰਙੀ ਸਬਦੁ ਸਦਾ ਧੁਨਿ ਸੋਹੈ ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਪੂਰੈ ਨਾਦੰ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: singee sabad sadaa dhun sohai ahinis poorai naadan |2|
lines.english: The sound of the horn ever rings out its beautiful melody, and day and night, he is filled with the sound current of the Naad. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੀਰਤਿ ਰਹਰਾਸਿ ਹਮਾਰੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੰਥੁ ਅਤੀਤੰ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: har keerat raharaas hamaaree guramukh panth ateetan |3|
lines.english: The Praise of the Lord is my occupation; and to live as Gurmukh is my pure religion. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਤੁ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ਗਿਆਨ ਮਤਿ ਡੰਡਾ ਵਰਤਮਾਨ ਬਿਭੂਤੰ ॥
lines.translit: pat veechaar giaan mat ddanddaa varatamaan bibhootan |
lines.english: My cup is reflective meditation, and spiritual wisdom is my walking stick; to dwell in the Lord's Presence is the ashes I apply to my body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਹਮਾਰੀ ਸੰਮਿਆ ਨਾਨਾ ਵਰਨ ਅਨੇਕੰ ॥
lines.translit: sagalee jot hamaaree samiaa naanaa varan anekan |
lines.english: My arm-rest is to see the Lord's Light in all, although their forms and colors are so numerous.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁੜੁ ਕਰਿ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਕਰਿ ਧਾਵੈ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਣੀ ਕਸੁ ਪਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: gurr kar giaan dhiaan kar dhaavai kar karanee kas paaeeai |
lines.english: Make spiritual wisdom your molasses, and meditation your scented flowers; let good deeds be the herbs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Aasaa, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਸੁਣਿ ਭਰਥਰਿ ਜੋਗੀ ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਲਿਵ ਏਕੰ ॥੪॥੩॥੩੭॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak sun bharathar jogee paarabraham liv ekan |4|3|37|
lines.english: Says Nanak, listen, O Bharthari Yogi: love only the Supreme Lord God. ||4||3||37||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਠੀ ਭਵਨੁ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਕਾ ਪੋਚਾ ਇਤੁ ਰਸਿ ਅਮਿਉ ਚੁਆਈਐ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: bhaatthee bhavan prem kaa pochaa it ras amiau chuaaeeai |1|
lines.english: Let devotional faith be the distilling fire, and your love the ceramic cup. Thus the sweet nectar of life is distilled. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਬਨੀ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗੀ ਸਬਦੁ ਅਨਾਹਦ ਗਹਿਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: ahinis banee prem liv laagee sabad anaahad gahiaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Night and day, remaining attached to the Love of the Lord, the celestial music of the Shabad resounds. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਬਾ ਮਨੁ ਮਤਵਾਰੋ ਨਾਮ ਰਸੁ ਪੀਵੈ ਸਹਜ ਰੰਗ ਰਚਿ ਰਹਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: baabaa man matavaaro naam ras peevai sehaj rang rach rahiaa |
lines.english: O Baba, the mind is intoxicated with the Naam, drinking in its Nectar. It remains absorbed in the Lord's Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਾ ਸਾਚੁ ਪਿਆਲਾ ਸਹਜੇ ਤਿਸਹਿ ਪੀਆਏ ਜਾ ਕਉ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: pooraa saach piaalaa sahaje tiseh peeae jaa kau nadar kare |
lines.english: The Perfect Lord naturally gives the cup of Truth, to the one upon whom He casts His Glance of Grace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸਾਖੀ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਾਣੀ ਪੀਵਤ ਹੀ ਪਰਵਾਣੁ ਭਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: gur kee saakhee amrit baanee peevat hee paravaan bheaa |
lines.english: The Teachings of the Guru, the Ambrosial Bani - drinking them in, one becomes acceptable and renowned.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕਾ ਵਾਪਾਰੀ ਹੋਵੈ ਕਿਆ ਮਦਿ ਛੂਛੈ ਭਾਉ ਧਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: amrit kaa vaapaaree hovai kiaa mad chhoochhai bhaau dhare |2|
lines.english: One who trades in this Nectar - how could he ever love the wine of the world? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰ ਦਰਸਨ ਕਾ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਬੈਕੁੰਠੈ ਕਰੈ ਕਿਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: dar darasan kaa preetam hovai mukat baikuntthai karai kiaa |3|
lines.english: Unto the one who loves the Lord's Court, and the Blessed Vision of His Darshan, of what use is liberation or paradise? ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਫਤੀ ਰਤਾ ਸਦ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ਜੂਐ ਜਨਮੁ ਨ ਹਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: sifatee rataa sad bairaagee jooai janam na haarai |
lines.english: Imbued with the Lord's Praises, one is forever a Bairaagee, a renunciate, and one's life is not lost in the gamble.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਸੁਣਿ ਭਰਥਰਿ ਜੋਗੀ ਖੀਵਾ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਧਾਰੈ ॥੪॥੪॥੩੮॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak sun bharathar jogee kheevaa amrit dhaarai |4|4|38|
lines.english: Says Nanak, listen, O Bharthari Yogi: drink in the intoxicating nectar of the Lord. ||4||4||38||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੈ ਦੋਸੁ ਨ ਦੇਈ ਕਰਤਾ ਜਮੁ ਕਰਿ ਮੁਗਲੁ ਚੜਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: aapai dos na deee karataa jam kar mugal charraaeaa |
lines.english: The Creator Himself does not take the blame, but has sent the Mugal as the messenger of death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੁਰਾਸਾਨ ਖਸਮਾਨਾ ਕੀਆ ਹਿੰਦੁਸਤਾਨੁ ਡਰਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: khuraasaan khasamaanaa keea hindusataan ddaraaeaa |
lines.english: Having attacked Khuraasaan, Baabar terrified Hindustan.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Aasaa, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਤਾ ਤੂੰ ਸਭਨਾ ਕਾ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: karataa toon sabhanaa kaa soee |
lines.english: O Creator Lord, You are the Master of all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਤੀ ਮਾਰ ਪਈ ਕਰਲਾਣੇ ਤੈਂ ਕੀ ਦਰਦੁ ਨ ਆਇਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: etee maar pee karalaane tain kee darad na aaeaa |1|
lines.english: There was so much slaughter that the people screamed. Didn't You feel compassion, Lord? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਕਤਾ ਸੀਹੁ ਮਾਰੇ ਪੈ ਵਗੈ ਖਸਮੈ ਸਾ ਪੁਰਸਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: sakataa seehu maare pai vagai khasamai saa purasaaee |
lines.english: But if a powerful tiger attacks a flock of sheep and kills them, then its master must answer for it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਸਕਤਾ ਸਕਤੇ ਕਉ ਮਾਰੇ ਤਾ ਮਨਿ ਰੋਸੁ ਨ ਹੋਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: je sakataa sakate kau maare taa man ros na hoee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: If some powerful man strikes out against another man, then no one feels any grief in their mind. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਤਨ ਵਿਗਾੜਿ ਵਿਗੋਏ ਕੁਤਂ‍ੀ ਮੁਇਆ ਸਾਰ ਨ ਕਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: ratan vigaarr vigoe kutanee mueaa saar na kaaee |
lines.english: This priceless country has been laid waste and defiled by dogs, and no one pays any attention to the dead.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਜੋੜਿ ਵਿਛੋੜੇ ਆਪੇ ਵੇਖੁ ਤੇਰੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: aape jorr vichhorre aape vekh teree vaddiaaee |2|
lines.english: You Yourself unite, and You Yourself separate; I gaze upon Your Glorious Greatness. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਕੋ ਨਾਉ ਧਰਾਏ ਵਡਾ ਸਾਦ ਕਰੇ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਣੇ ॥
lines.translit: je ko naau dharaae vaddaa saad kare man bhaane |
lines.english: One may give himself a great name, and revel in the pleasures of the mind,

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਮੈ ਨਦਰੀ ਕੀੜਾ ਆਵੈ ਜੇਤੇ ਚੁਗੈ ਦਾਣੇ ॥
lines.translit: khasamai nadaree keerraa aavai jete chugai daane |
lines.english: but in the Eyes of the Lord and Master, he is just a worm, for all the corn that he eats.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਰਿ ਮਰਿ ਜੀਵੈ ਤਾ ਕਿਛੁ ਪਾਏ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਖਾਣੇ ॥੩॥੫॥੩੯॥
lines.translit: mar mar jeevai taa kichh paae naanak naam vakhaane |3|5|39|
lines.english: Only one who dies to his ego while yet alive, obtains the blessings, O Nanak, by chanting the Lord's Name. ||3||5||39||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਆਸਾ ਘਰੁ ੨ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: raag aasaa ghar 2 mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Raag Aasaa, Second House, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਚੈ ਬੈਰਾਗਿ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai sabad sachai bairaag |
lines.english: Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, true detachment is obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਪਾਵੈ ਵਡਭਾਗਿ ॥
lines.translit: har darasan paavai vaddabhaag |
lines.english: The Blessed Vision of the Lord's Darshan is obtained by great good fortune.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਟੁ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਵਰਤੈ ਵਰਤਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: khatt darasan varatai varataaraa |
lines.english: The six systems of philosophy are pervasive,
